content.claim: Between-school variance in Hungary is the third highest among OECD countries; socio-economic background accounts for 23.1% of student performance variance, compared to OECD average of 14.8%, and the school-level correlation between disadvantage share and Roma student share is 0.79
content.context: Fresh live research through the Finnish system reform lens
limitations: Data year(s) not specified in research notes
Causality between tracking system design and variance levels not directly tested in these statistics alone
Roma segregation measure (0.79 correlation) may reflect multiple institutional and social factors beyond school selection policy
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
